retrieved_kb_context:
## Retrieved KB Context for LLM Planning
Use these similar projects as calibration signals for task scope, FR/NFR classification, complexity levels, and dependency hints. Do not copy tasks unless they are implied by the new requirements.
Hard constraints for this run: emit one task per requirement line, do not merge repeated source lines, keep security/performance/compliance/availability/mobile/localization/privacy constraints as NFRs, and keep titles action-oriented (Implement/Integrate for FR; Enforce/Optimize/Enable for NFR).

### FINTECH project, critic_score=0.92:
  - Implement KYC Verification Workflow (FR, C4)
  - Implement Wallet Funding Flow (FR, C4)
  - Integrate Payment Gateway (FR, C4)
  - Implement Fraud Review Queue (FR, C4)
  - Implement Settlement Reporting (FR, C3)
  - Implement Customer Notification Events (FR, C3)
  - PCI-DSS Security Controls NFR (NFR, C5)
  - Payment Service Availability NFR (NFR, C4)

### CRM project, critic_score=0.91:
  - Implement Lead CRUD and Status Tracking (FR, C3)
  - Implement Email Automation Integration (FR, C4)
  - Implement Sales Pipeline Dashboard (FR, C3)
  - Implement Contact Search and Filter (FR, C2)
  - Implement Sales Reports and Export (FR, C3)
  - Role-Based Access Control NFR (NFR, C3)
  - System Uptime and Reliability NFR (NFR, C3)

Apply similar FR/NFR classification and complexity levels to the new requirements below, while preserving exact source references and the one-line-to-one-task mapping.

risk_signals: External integrations are present and will need interface contracts plus vendor coordination.
Offline capability requires secure local storage, synchronization, and recovery-path validation.
Compliance-oriented requirements are present and should be validated early to avoid late rework.
Non-functional scope is substantial, so quality engineering should run in parallel with feature delivery.
11 high-complexity tasks sit on the roadmap and deserve early prototyping.
Some non-root workflow tasks are still structurally isolated and should be reviewed before scheduling.

committee_brief.domain_inference: Likely domain: fintech, payments, and billing operations.
committee_brief.scope_assessment: Scope includes 11 functional task(s) and 23 non-functional task(s); 0 task(s) sit outside the confirmed baseline because they are optional or low-confidence.
assumption_log: Dependency sequencing was inferred for 12 task(s) to produce an executable implementation order beyond the raw prose order.
committee_brief.confidence_signal: Planning confidence is high: no low-confidence tasks were detected in the current plan.
committee_brief.graph_summary: 33 requirements -> 34 atomic tasks across 7 delivery sprints. Critical delivery path: T015 -> T001 -> T023 -> T004 -> T005 -> T006 -> T008 (7 hops, ~20 story points, estimated 16 weeks with a 2-person team). Coordination hotspot: Enforce WCAG AA accessibility requirements blocks 10 downstream tasks and should be closed by Sprint 1. Parallel execution opportunity: 22 tasks can start immediately in Sprint 1. Operational constraint: 1 offline requirement should be validated for secure caching, synchronization, and recovery behavior before release. Effort breakdown: 1070 estimated hours | FR: 285h | NFR: 785h.
